0.description: Corvin's private study on the second sublevel. A gentleman's office rendered grotesque by context -- leather armchairs, a mahogany desk, and a crystal decanter of brandy, all within earshot of the temple below. He conducts politics and murder from the same chair.
0.read_aloud: Corvin's desk is organized with the precision of a man who controls every detail. A leather appointment book. A telephone. Correspondence from the mayor's office. And on a side table, as casually displayed as a family photograph: a ceremonial dagger with a blade that doesn't reflect light.

enemies.name: Cult Guards
enemies.notes: Corvin's personal guards, stationed in the study. Blood-enhanced and fanatically loyal. They've witnessed the rituals and believe the Covenant is the future of civilization.
special: Blood-Enhanced: advantage on first attack each round
Fanatical: fight to the death, cannot be intimidated

0.investigation_success: The appointment book contains coded entries. Decoded using Lyssa's cipher: Corvin has been meeting privately with the thinning for months. Not performing rituals -- having conversations. The thinning is intelligent and it's been coaching Corvin on the Unabridged transformation. Corvin isn't leading the cult. The thinning is.
event_triggers: A twist: Corvin isn't the mastermind. The thinning itself has been manipulating the cult, guiding them toward the Unabridged ritual for its own purposes. Corvin is a puppet who thinks he's the puppeteer.

1.description: The correspondence vault. A room of locked filing cabinets containing three years of blackmail material, political leverage, and evidence of the cult's secular crimes. Enough to destroy careers even without the supernatural element.
1.read_aloud: Corvin didn't just recruit followers -- he compromised them. Every cult member's darkest secret is catalogued here. Financial fraud. Affairs. A judge who accepted bribes. A senator who falsified military records. The cult isn't held together by belief. It's held together by mutual destruction.

enemies.name: Blood Magick Ward
enemies.notes: A protective ward on the filing cabinets. When triggered, it floods the room with bleed energy -- a defensive measure Corvin installed to protect his leverage.
special: Bleed Pulse: all investigators in the room take 1 Bleed mark per round
Disarmed by disrupting the ward sigil inscribed on the floor beneath the cabinets

loot.name: Blackmail Archive
loot.description: Complete dossiers on all seven cult members, documenting both their supernatural and secular crimes. Enough to end political careers, but using it publicly would expose Candela's investigation.

loot.name: Stake Case Key
loot.description: A small iron key, warded against bleed alarm. Opens the glass case containing the Seven Stakes of Ire without triggering the alert. Hidden in a false bottom of Corvin's desk drawer.

2.description: The containment cell. Behind the locked door from the preparation chamber: a small room with a cot, a bucket, and a person. The surviving sacrifice -- alive, confused, and exhibiting signs of bleed resistance that the cult found inconvenient.
2.read_aloud: She looks up when the door opens. Young, mid-twenties, wrists bruised from restraints, eyes red from crying but dry now. 'Are you with them?' she asks. 'Because if you are, you should know -- whatever they did to me, it didn't work. And now I can see things. The blood on the walls. It's moving. It's always moving.'

npcs.name: Senna Park
npcs.dialogue: They drugged me at a charity gala. I woke up on the slab. They cut me and the blood flowed into the channels but the thing -- the red thing in the air -- it spat me back. Rejected. Like I tasted wrong. Since then I can see it. The bleed. It's everywhere in this building, in the walls, in their eyes. Corvin's eyes are the worst. There's something behind his eyes that isn't him.
npcs.notes: Senna developed bleed-sight from the failed sacrifice -- she can see bleed energy and entities. A rare and valuable ability. She can confirm that Corvin is partially Unabridged and identify which other cult members have begun the transformation.

event_triggers: Senna Park, the surviving sacrifice, has developed bleed-sight. She can see the thinning's influence throughout the estate -- and she can confirm Corvin is no longer fully human.

3.description: The ritual preparation room. Seven ceremonial robes hang on hooks. Seven brass chalices sit on a shelf. A basin of water and a mirror -- the cult's cleansing ritual before ceremonies. But the mirror doesn't work correctly anymore.
3.read_aloud: You look in the mirror and see yourself, but behind your reflection, the room continues deeper than it should -- a long corridor stretching into red darkness. Figures move in the corridor. They're wearing the cult's robes. They're walking toward the thinning. Their reflections have no faces.
3.perception_success: The mirror is a bleed artifact -- not intentionally, but contaminated by proximity to the thinning. It shows the bleed-reflection of whoever looks into it: what you would become if you stepped through. For the cult members, their reflections are already Unabridged. The process has begun whether they complete the ritual or not.
